raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The consolidation of local news by large conglomerates threatens the foundation of American democracy.",
    "Media chains managed by hedge funds and private equity have a track record of reducing costs and maximizing profits by gutting local newspapers.",
    "Layoffs, closures, and consolidations of newsrooms deprive the public of quality local news coverage.",
    "In 2024, just 10 companies controlled over half of all daily newspapers nationwide in the U.S.",
    "The concentration of newsroom ownership by media chains, private equity, and hedge funds leads to severe loss of professional expertise in local news coverage.",
    "Gannett and MediaNews Group own newsrooms across California, including the Mercury News and Desert Sun.",
    "Investment firms like Alden Global Capital have acquired numerous local newspapers across the U.S.",
    "The acquisition of local newspapers by hedge funds results in buyouts and staff layoffs.",
    "The severe loss of professional expertise in local journalism leads to gaps in local news coverage.",
    "Residents become less informed about local policies and elections that affect their daily lives due to the consolidation of local news.",
    "Voter turnout declines and elections become less competitive as a result of decreased local news coverage.",
    "Syndicated news tends to focus more on national rather than local politics, which deepens polarization and partisan politics.",
    "Preserving independent, high-quality local journalism is essential to ensuring informed, engaged, and empowered communities.",
    "AB 611 promotes employee and small business ownership of newsrooms by requiring local media outlets to provide notice before selling.",
    "The consolidation of local newspapers by hedge funds leads to a lack of access to information that helps the public vote and hold power accountable."
  ]
}
```
